label: NIST
description: National Institute of Standards and Technology — US federal agency that standardized the first PQC algorithms
notes: NIST's PQC Standardization Project (launched 2016) selected ML-KEM (FIPS 203), ML-DSA (FIPS 204), and SLH-DSA (FIPS 205) as the first post-quantum standards, published August 2024. FN-DSA (FIPS 206) published March 2025. NIST IR 8547 defines the transition timeline: deprecate 112-bit classical asymmetric by 2030, disallow by 2035. NIST also runs CMVP (module validation) and CAVP (algorithm validation) programs that enforce these standards globally.

label: ENISA
description: European Union Agency for Cybersecurity — EU's PQC guidance authority and EUCC scheme co-manager
notes: ENISA published the landmark 2022 Post-Quantum Cryptography report and subsequent PQC guidelines adopted across EU member states. Co-manages the EUCC certification scheme with ECCG. Provides technical support for NIS2 implementation and coordinates EU-wide PQC migration readiness. ENISA's guidelines are the primary reference for EU organizations planning PQC transitions.

label: ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 27
description: ISO/IEC Joint Technical Committee 1, Subcommittee 27 — international body standardizing PQC algorithms for global adoption
notes: ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 27 Working Group 2 standardizes cryptographic mechanisms for global use. Key PQC work items: ISO/IEC 14888-4 (ML-DSA/Dilithium digital signatures, under development), ISO/IEC 18033 series (encryption, ML-KEM alignment). ISO standards often harmonize with NIST outputs to enable global adoption outside US jurisdiction. Relevant for organizations requiring internationally recognized standards beyond NIST FIPS.

label: IETF
description: Internet Engineering Task Force — open standards body integrating PQC algorithms into internet protocols (TLS, SSH, CMS, S/MIME)
notes: IETF's PQUIP (Post-Quantum Use In Protocols) working group coordinates PQC integration across the internet protocol stack. 30+ active RFCs and drafts for PQC in 2025-2026 including: RFC 9881/9882 (ML-DSA in X.509/CMS), RFC 9794 (hybrid terminology), RFC 9814 (SLH-DSA in CMS), draft-ietf-tls-ecdhe-mlkem (TLS 1.3 hybrid), draft-ietf-lamps-pq-composite-kem (composite KEM). 6+ drafts in RFC Editor Queue as of early 2026.

label: FIPS 140-3
description: NIST cryptographic module validation program
notes: CMVP validation will require PQC algorithm support. FIPS 203/204/205 already published. The 2030 date reflects NIST IR 8547's timeline for deprecating 112-bit classical asymmetric algorithms. Important milestone: all FIPS 140-2 certificates move to the Historical list on September 21 2026 — after that date only FIPS 140-3 validated modules may be procured for new federal systems.

label: Common Criteria
description: International security evaluation framework
notes: New protection profiles for PQC are being developed. Certification timelines vary by scheme.

label: ANSSI
description: French national cybersecurity agency regulation
notes: French regulation requires hybrid PQC for new systems. Full migration by 2030. ANSSI issued first PQC security certifications Sept-Oct 2025 (Thales Smartcard MultiApp 5.2 Premium PQC, Samsung S3SSE2A). Updated PQC FAQ published Oct 2025 with comprehensive algorithm guidance. Mandatory PQC qualification requirement effective 2027.
library_refs: ANSSI PQC Position Paper;ANSSI PQC Follow-up Paper;ANSSI-PQC-FAQ-2025

label: CNSA 2.0
description: NSA mandate for national security systems
notes: NSA mandate for U.S. national security systems. Phased deadlines by category: software/firmware signing prefer 2025 exclusively 2030; web/cloud prefer 2025 exclusively 2033; networking prefer 2026 exclusively 2030; OS prefer 2027 exclusively 2033; niche/legacy 2030-2033. Revised May 2025 algorithm document specifies ML-KEM-1024, ML-DSA-87, AES-256, SHA-384/512, LMS/XMSS. Executive Order 14306 (June 2025) ties CNSA 2.0 to federal procurement mandates via CISA product category list.
library_refs: NSA CNSA 2.0;NSA CNSA 2.0 FAQ;NSA CSfC PQC Guidance Addendum;EO-14306;DoD-CIO-PQC-Memo-2025

label: eIDAS 2.0
description: EU Digital Identity and Trust Services regulation
notes: EU Digital Identity framework (Regulation 2024/1183, effective May 2024). Member states have 24 months to deploy at least one national wallet — targeting May 2026. EUDI Wallet ARF specifies PQC migration roadmap: Dec 2026 national PQC roadmaps, Dec 2030 high-risk migration, Dec 2035 full PQC transition. ENISA wallet security analysis identifies wallet providers as high-impact targets for early PQC adoption.

label: PCI DSS
description: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
notes: PCI DSS 4.0 Requirement 12.3.3 (mandatory since March 2025) requires documented cryptographic cipher suite inventory and a plan for anticipated crypto changes — effectively a crypto-agility mandate. Payment card industry will follow NIST guidance for PQC algorithm specifics.

label: HIPAA
description: US health data privacy and security rule
notes: Healthcare data has long sensitivity periods — HNDL risk is significant.

label: GSMA NG.116 / FS.40
description: GSMA PQC guidelines for mobile network operators
notes: Mobile network operator PQC guidance from GSMA. Covers 5G and beyond.

label: ETSI TS 103 744
description: ETSI quantum-safe cryptography for telecommunications
notes: ETSI specification for quantum-safe algorithms in telecom protocols.

label: DORA (EU Digital Operational Resilience)
description: EU financial sector ICT risk and resilience regulation
notes: Operational resilience requirements for EU financial entities. Crypto risk in scope.

label: SWIFT CSP
description: SWIFT Customer Security Programme mandatory controls
notes: Annual security attestation required for SWIFT network participants.

label: ISO 27001
description: International information security management standard
notes: Widely adopted ISMS standard. No explicit PQC deadline but requires crypto risk management.

label: SOC 2
description: Service Organization Control 2 trust services criteria
notes: Trust Services Criteria. Cryptographic controls reviewed in SOC 2 Type II audits.

label: GDPR
description: EU General Data Protection Regulation
notes: Art. 32 requires appropriate encryption. PQC becoming expected standard for long-lived personal data.

label: HITECH Act
description: Health Information Technology for Economic and Clinical Health Act
notes: Extends HIPAA enforcement. Mandates breach notification and strong encryption for ePHI.

label: FDA 21 CFR Part 11
description: Electronic records and signatures in FDA-regulated industries
notes: Signature integrity requirements applicable to PQC migration for regulated medical software.

label: FedRAMP
description: Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program
notes: Cloud service authorization for U.S. federal agencies. Rev 5 (2023) updated baseline controls; explicit PQC requirements are expected in forthcoming guidance.

label: DISA STIGs
description: Defense Information Systems Agency Security Technical Implementation Guides
notes: Mandatory hardening guides for U.S. DoD systems. Crypto requirements updated regularly.

label: NATO STANAG 4774
description: NATO confidentiality metadata labeling standard
notes: Coalition partner interoperability requirements. PQC transition affects classified data exchange.

label: ISO/SAE 21434
description: Road vehicle cybersecurity engineering standard
notes: Full vehicle lifecycle security including post-quantum readiness. Required for new type approvals.

label: UN ECE WP.29 R155/R156
description: UN regulations for vehicle cybersecurity and software updates
notes: Type approval requires cybersecurity management systems and secure OTA update processes.

label: NERC CIP
description: North American Electric Reliability Corporation Critical Infrastructure Protection
notes: Mandatory cybersecurity standards for bulk power systems. Crypto controls in CIP-005/007/011.

label: IEC 62443
description: Industrial cybersecurity standard for OT/ICS environments
notes: Defense-in-depth for industrial control systems. PQC relevant for long-lived OT deployments.

label: DO-326A / ED-202A
description: Airworthiness security process specification for aircraft systems
notes: Aviation cybersecurity certification basis. PQC critical given 30-40 year aircraft lifespans.

label: RTCA DO-355A
description: Information security guidance for continuing airworthiness
notes: Supplement to DO-326A for operational phase. Covers cryptographic controls in service.

label: FERPA
description: Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act
notes: Student record confidentiality. Long retention periods imply HNDL exposure for sensitive records.

label: NIS2 Directive
description: EU network and information security directive for essential and important entities
notes: EU-wide cybersecurity baseline for essential and important entities. Member states were required to transpose NIS2 by October 2024; enforcement is now active across the EU.

label: COPPA
description: US Children's Online Privacy Protection Act
notes: Protects children under 13 online. PQC relevant for long-lived minor data and parental consent records.

label: TISAX
description: Trusted Information Security Assessment Exchange for automotive supply chain
notes: VDA-managed assessment for automotive suppliers. Covers information security including cryptographic controls.

label: MiCA
description: EU Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation
notes: EU regulation for crypto-asset service providers (cryptocurrencies and digital tokens). MiCA itself is not a cryptography standard, but security requirements for digital wallets and custody systems are indirectly affected by PQC migration. Stablecoin titles applied from June 30 2024; full regulation from December 30 2024.

label: TSA Pipeline Security Directive
description: US TSA cybersecurity requirements for pipeline operators
notes: Mandatory cybersecurity measures for critical pipeline infrastructure. Covers encryption for SCADA/OT.

label: NIST IR 8547
description: NIST transition to post-quantum cryptography standards guidance
notes: Transition guidance recommending deprecation of 112-bit classical asymmetric algorithms by 2030 and disallowance by 2035.

label: ASD ISM
description: Australian Signals Directorate Information Security Manual
notes: ISM control ISM-1917 requiring ML-DSA-87, ML-KEM-1024, SHA-384/512, AES-256. Full PQC transition by 2030.

label: CCCS ITSM.40.001
description: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security roadmap for PQC migration
notes: Roadmap for migration to PQC for Canadian government systems.

label: EU Recommendation 2024/1101
description: European Commission coordinated PQC implementation roadmap
notes: Coordinated implementation roadmap for PQC transition across EU member states. NIS Cooperation Group Roadmap v1.1 (June 2025) provides harmonized timelines and cross-border coordination.

label: BSI TR-02102
description: German BSI technical guideline for cryptographic mechanisms, TLS, IPsec, SSH
notes: BSI technical guideline series covering PQC recommendations including ML-KEM and ML-DSA. v2026-01 recommends hybrid PQC now; classical-only Diffie-Hellman key agreement recommended only until end of 2031.

label: UK NCSC PQC Guidance
description: UK National Cyber Security Centre quantum-safe cryptography guidance
notes: Three-phase roadmap: Phase 1 (now-2028) identify crypto services and build migration plans; Phase 2 (2028-2031) execute high-priority upgrades; Phase 3 (2031-2035) complete full migration to PQC across all systems.

label: CRYPTREC
description: Japan Cryptography Research and Evaluation Committees PQC guidelines
notes: Japanese government cryptographic evaluation body publishing PQC guidelines and preparing detailed transition guidance.

label: MAS Circular
description: Monetary Authority of Singapore financial services PQC advisory
notes: MAS/TCRS/2024/01 requiring Singapore financial services to prepare PQC transition plans.

label: KpqC Standardization
description: Korea Post-Quantum Cryptography standardization program with national PQC transition roadmap
notes: KpqC Round 1 winners (Jan 2025): HAETAE, AIMer, SMAUG-T, NTRU+. MSIT pilot phase 2025-2028.

label: KCMVP
description: Korean Cryptographic Module Validation Program for government systems
notes: Mandatory module validation for Korean government. PQC algorithm integration expected after KpqC standardization completes (~2027-2028).

label: NZISM
description: New Zealand Information Security Manual with PQC preparation requirements
notes: NZISM v3.9 (Nov 2025) Section 2.4 on PQC preparation. 19 agencies implementing. Aligned with Five Eyes.

label: INCD PQC Guidance
description: Israel National Cyber Directorate post-quantum readiness guidance
notes: PQC readiness guide published Jan 2022. Government ministries must complete quantum threat assessment and require PQC in new contracts by end 2025.

label: BOI Quantum Risk Directive
description: Bank of Israel directive requiring financial institutions to manage quantum risk
notes: Directive Letter 202501EN requiring quantum risk management including HNDL exposure assessment and PQC transition planning.

label: OSCCA NGCC Program
description: China Next-Generation Commercial Cryptographic algorithms standardization program
notes: NGCC call for proposals launched Feb 2025 by ICCS. China developing independent PQC algorithms to supplement SM-series. Financial sector migration estimated 2027-2030.
library_refs: China OSCCA GM/T 0108-2021;China GB/T 42829-2023;China GB/T 43692-2024;China CACR PQC Competition Results;China ICCS PQC Call for Proposals

label: Swiss NCSC PQC Guidance
description: Swiss National Cyber Security Centre post-quantum cryptography migration recommendations
notes: NCSC Switzerland published PQC migration recommendations for the federal government and critical infrastructure operators. Aligned with NIST FIPS 203/204/205 and European guidance from BSI/ANSSI. No mandatory hard deadline; migration is recommended to begin now.

label: NCSC-NL PQC Guidance
description: Dutch National Cyber Security Centre post-quantum cryptography migration guide
notes: NCSC-NL published a comprehensive PQC factsheet and migration guide for Dutch government and organisations. Covers algorithm selection aligned with NIST PQC standards and transition planning. Guidance rather than mandatory regulation.

label: EUCC
description: EU Cybersecurity Certification Scheme for ICT products based on Common Criteria
notes: Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2024/482 establishes EUCC as the EU-wide ICT product security certification scheme, replacing fragmented national Common Criteria schemes. Three assurance levels: basic (AVA_VAN.1/2), substantial (AVA_VAN.3), high (AVA_VAN.4/5). Cryptographic requirements are defined in the Agreed Cryptographic Mechanisms (ACM) — v2.0 (April 2025) added PQC algorithms including ML-KEM and ML-DSA. National certification authorities (NCAs) in each EU member state issue EUCC certificates.

label: ANSSI
description: Agence nationale de la sécurité des systèmes d'information — France's national cybersecurity authority
notes: ANSSI is France's primary cybersecurity authority, publishing PQC guidance since 2022. Key publications include the 2022 PQC Position Paper and 2024 Follow-Up Paper recommending hybrid PQC for new systems. ANSSI coordinates with ENISA and BSI on European PQC harmonization.
library_refs: ANSSI PQC Position Paper;ANSSI PQC Follow-up Paper

label: CRYPTREC
description: Cryptography Research and Evaluation Committees — Japan's government body evaluating cryptographic techniques for public and government use
notes: CRYPTREC is a joint committee of Japan's Ministry of Internal Affairs (MIC) and METI. Publishes the CRYPTREC Ciphers List (approved algorithms for e-Government systems). Active PQC evaluation programme aligned with NIST outcomes. Annual technical reports cover PQC migration readiness.

label: KISA / NIS
description: Korea Internet & Security Agency and National Intelligence Service — South Korea's cybersecurity and cryptography standardization authorities
notes: KISA (under MSIT) leads Korean internet security and runs the KpqC competition for domestic PQC algorithms. NIS oversees KCMVP (Korean Cryptographic Module Validation Program). Together they define cryptographic requirements for government systems and are driving Korea's PQC transition roadmap targeting 2035.

label: OSCCA / SCA
description: Office of State Commercial Cryptography Administration (国家密码管理局) — China's regulatory authority for commercial cryptography
notes: OSCCA (now operating as SCA — State Cryptography Administration) regulates all commercial cryptography in China. Administers the SM-series algorithms (SM2/SM3/SM4) as national standards. Launched the NGCC (Next-Generation Commercial Cryptographic) call for proposals in Feb 2025 to develop China-indigenous PQC algorithms alongside NIST-aligned ones.

label: ETSI
description: European Telecommunications Standards Institute — global standards body for telecoms PQC including quantum-safe algorithm suites for 5G/6G
notes: ETSI's Quantum Safe Cryptography (QSC) working group (TC CYBER WG QSC) publishes technical reports and specifications for PQC in telecoms. Key outputs: ETSI TS 103 744 (QSC for telecoms), ETSI TR 103 619 (migration guidance), ETSI GR QSC 001 (quantum threats survey). Works in liaison with NIST, ISO/IEC and 3GPP SA3.

label: BSI
description: German Federal Office for Information Security — Europe's most detailed national PQC technical guidance authority
notes: BSI (Bundesamt für Sicherheit in der Informationstechnik) publishes the TR-02102 series — the most technically detailed national PQC cryptographic guidelines globally. Coordinates with ANSSI and ENISA on European PQC harmonization. BSI issues PQC product certifications under the national CC scheme and the EUCC scheme.

label: NSA
description: US National Security Agency — mandates PQC for all national security systems via CNSA 2.0 with the most aggressive government timeline
notes: NSA's CNSA 2.0 (Commercial National Security Algorithm Suite 2.0) mandates ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, and LMS/XMSS for all US national security systems. Phased deadlines by category: software/firmware signing prefer 2025 exclusively 2030; web/cloud 2025/2033; networking 2026/2030; OS 2027/2033; legacy 2030-2033. NSA also issues CSfC (Commercial Solutions for Classified) addenda incorporating PQC guidance.
library_refs: NSA CNSA 2.0;NSA CNSA 2.0 FAQ;NSA CSfC PQC Guidance Addendum

label: CMMC
description: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification — US DoD supply chain cybersecurity certification requirement
notes: CMMC 2.0 (effective since Dec 2024) requires cybersecurity practices for DoD contractors. Cryptographic controls align with NIST SP 800-171 and FIPS 140-3. PQC requirements expected as NIST SP 800-171 is updated to align with NIST IR 8547 transition guidance.

label: FISMA
description: Federal Information Security Modernization Act — mandates information security programs for all US federal agencies
notes: FISMA requires federal agencies to follow NIST security standards including cryptographic controls. As NIST SP 800-53 and NIST IR 8547 transition guidance are updated, FISMA compliance will require PQC migration by 2030. OMB Memorandum M-23-02 already references quantum risk. FedRAMP (cloud) and DISA STIGs (DoD) both derive from FISMA.

label: EU Cyber Resilience Act
description: EU regulation establishing cybersecurity requirements for connected products throughout their lifecycle
notes: Regulation (EU) 2024/2847 — the EU Cyber Resilience Act mandates security-by-design for products with digital elements sold in the EU. Cryptographic requirements apply to connected devices over their entire support lifecycle. PQC relevance: devices shipped before 2030 must remain secure through 2040+ — HNDL risk makes PQC-readiness a CRA requirement. Applies to manufacturers, importers, and distributors.

label: India DST/NQM PQC Roadmap
description: India National Quantum Mission post-quantum cryptography migration roadmap for all sectors
notes: India DST/NQM published comprehensive national PQC roadmap (Feb 2026) with mandatory CBOM submissions starting FY 2027-28. Critical Information Infrastructure migration by 2029. Full nationwide PQC adoption by 2033. Hybrid PQC plus QKD recommended for high-assurance applications.

label: HKMA Fintech 2030 Quantum Preparedness
description: Hong Kong Monetary Authority quantum preparedness framework for financial institutions
notes: HKMA Fintech 2030 Quantum Preparedness Index requires all regulated banks to complete formal quantum preparedness assessment by 2027 covering cryptographic inventory, CBOM, and migration roadmap. Full PQC migration target by 2030 aligned with BIS and MAS timelines. Critical Infrastructure Cybersecurity Ordinance effective Jan 2026 covers 8 sectors.

label: UAE National Encryption Policy
description: UAE Cybersecurity Council national encryption policy mandating PQC transition
notes: UAE Cybersecurity Council approved National Encryption Policy (Nov 2025) mandating all government entities develop PQC transition plans. Migration roadmaps due 2026. QuantumGate program drives large-scale PQC implementation 2026-2030. DESC launched PQC Guideline at GISEC Global 2025.

label: CISA PQC Federal Buying Guidance
description: CISA product category list and federal procurement guidance for PQC per EO 14306
notes: CISA published PQC product category list per Executive Order 14306 identifying cloud services, web software, networking hardware, and endpoint security as PQC-ready categories. Federal agencies directed to acquire only PQC-capable products. Developed with NSA and ATARC.

label: G7 Financial Sector PQC Roadmap
description: G7 Cyber Expert Group coordinated PQC transition roadmap for the financial sector
notes: G7 Cyber Expert Group (chaired by US Treasury and Bank of England) published coordinated PQC transition framework for global financial sector (Jan 2026). Covers inventory, risk prioritization, algorithm selection, testing, and interoperability for G7 financial institutions.

label: PKCS#11 v3.2 (PQC HSM Interface)
description: OASIS PKCS#11 v3.2 standard adding ML-KEM, ML-DSA, and SLH-DSA mechanisms for hardware security modules
notes: PKCS#11 v3.2 adds PQC mechanisms: ML-KEM (CKM_ML_KEM), ML-DSA (CKM_ML_DSA), SLH-DSA (CKM_SLH_DSA). Introduces C_EncapsulateKey()/C_DecapsulateKey() for KEM operations. New key types and parameter set attributes. Critical for all HSM vendors implementing PQC.

label: CA/Browser Forum Ballot SMC013
description: CA/Browser Forum ballot enabling ML-DSA PQC signatures in S/MIME certificates
notes: Enables ML-DSA post-quantum digital signatures in S/MIME certificates. IPR exclusion period completed with no notices. First Web PKI governance body PQC adoption, signaling CAs can issue PQC S/MIME certificates.

label: NIST ACVP (Algorithm Validation)
description: NIST Automated Cryptographic Validation Protocol for PQC algorithm testing
notes: NIST CAVP/ACVP validates individual cryptographic algorithm implementations. Already validating ML-KEM and ML-DSA implementations. Distinct from CMVP/FIPS 140-3 which validates complete modules. Provides algorithm-level compliance evidence.

label: Taiwan Five-Year PQC Plan
description: Taiwan Ministry of Digital Affairs five-year PQC implementation plan
notes: Taiwan MODA launched five-year PQC implementation plan (2024-2029) targeting quantum-safe government digital infrastructure. Interim quantum-safe milestone at 2027.

label: Malaysia National PQC Migration
description: Malaysia NACSA national PQC migration roadmap — first ASEAN nation with comprehensive plan
notes: First ASEAN nation to publish comprehensive national PQC migration roadmap (Oct 2025). Includes PQC Sandbox program for proof-of-concept testing. Phased approach aligned with NIST standards.

label: Saudi NCA Cybersecurity Controls
description: Saudi Arabia National Cybersecurity Authority essential cybersecurity controls with expanded cryptography domain
notes: NCA released Essential Cybersecurity Controls 2024 (ECC-2:2024) with expanded cryptography domain covering encryption strength, key management, secure protocols, and lifecycle governance. National quantum strategy with M budget aligned with Vision 2030.

label: BIS Paper 158 Quantum Roadmap
description: Bank for International Settlements quantum-readiness roadmap for global financial systems
notes: BIS Paper 158 outlines quantum-readiness roadmap for global financial systems covering cryptographic inventory, migration timelines, and cross-border interoperability for central banks. Referenced by MAS, HKMA, and G7 CEG.

label: 3GPP PQC for 5G/6G
description: 3GPP SA3 study on integrating PQC into 5G security protocols
notes: 3GPP SA3 study (TR 33.841) on integrating standalone or hybrid PQC algorithms into existing 5G security protocols including TLS, IPSec, and IKEv2. Complements GSMA NG.116 and ETSI QSC work.

label: TCG TPM 2.0 PQC Spec
description: Trusted Computing Group TPM 2.0 library specification update incorporating PQC algorithms
notes: TPM 2.0 Library specification updates incorporating FIPS 203/204 PQC algorithms with hybrid classical+PQ support. Aligns with TPM 2.0 v1.85 standard. Affects every PC, server, and embedded device using hardware root of trust.

label: NIST NCCoE SP 1800-38 Migration Guide
description: NIST NCCoE practical enterprise PQC migration guide with reference architectures
notes: NIST NCCoE SP 1800-38 series (Volumes A/B/C) provides practical enterprise PQC migration guidance with use cases and reference architectures. CSWP 48 maps migration capabilities to CSF 2.0 and SP 800-53 controls.

label: NIST SP 800-131A Rev 3
description: NIST algorithm deprecation roadmap — SHA-1 and weak RSA/ECC disallowed after 2030
notes: Federal algorithm retirement calendar. SHA-1 and RSA/ECC below 128-bit security disallowed after 2030. Establishes FIPS 203/204/205 as post-2030 replacements. Distinct from IR 8547 in covering all algorithm deprecation not just PQC transition.

label: India CERT-In CBOM/QBOM Guidelines
description: India CERT-In expanded Bill of Materials requirements including Cryptographic BOM and Quantum Readiness BOM
notes: CERT-In expands Bill of Materials requirements to include Cryptographic BOM (CBOM) and Quantum Readiness BOM (QBOM). First national CBOM mandate from any country. Part of India broader PQC migration framework under National Quantum Mission.

label: Italy ACN PQC Guidance
description: Italy Agenzia per la Cybersicurezza Nazionale post-quantum cryptography guidance
notes: ACN published PQC guidance (July 2024) covering quantum threat landscape and migration recommendations. Italy also participates in BIS Project Leap Phase 2 testing hybrid PQC on TARGET2 payment system.

label: Spain CCN Four-Phase PQC
description: Spain Centro Criptologico Nacional four-phase PQC transition approach
notes: CCN defines four-phase PQC approach from current state through post-2030 full transition. Recommends NIST algorithms and FrodoKEM. Active since 2022.

label: Bahrain Quantum-Safe Deployment
description: Bahrain National Cyber Security Centre quantum-safe cybersecurity deployment across government
notes: One of the world first large-scale national quantum-safe commitments. Deployed AQtive Guard across 60+ government ministries for automated cryptographic discovery and PQC transition readiness.

label: Jordan Financial Sector PQC Roadmap
description: Central Bank of Jordan sector-wide roadmap for transitioning banks to quantum-resistant encryption
notes: Central Bank of Jordan published sector-wide PQC roadmap (Jan 2026) with phased approach: cryptographic asset cataloging, risk-based prioritization, governance integration, sandbox testing, and full transition.

label: UK CMORG Financial PQC Guidance
description: UK Cross Market Operational Resilience Group PQC guidance for financial sector
notes: CMORG (convened by Bank of England) published PQC guidance specifically for UK financial sector covering threat landscape, algorithm selection, migration planning, and prioritization. Complements UK NCSC guidance with financial sector focus.

label: Europol Quantum Safe Financial Forum
description: Europol QSFF call to action for financial sector PQC migration
notes: Europol Quantum Safe Financial Forum found 86% of financial executives unprepared for PQC. Published joint guidance with FS-ISAC (Jan 2026) introducing Quantum Risk Score framework for migration prioritization.

label: PQCC Migration Roadmap
description: Post-Quantum Cryptography Coalition cross-sector PQC migration framework
notes: PQCC (founded by IBM, Microsoft, MITRE, PQShield, SandboxAQ) published PQC Migration Roadmap (May 2025) with four-category framework and PQC Inventory Workbook (June 2025). Becoming a de facto industry reference for enterprise PQC migration.

label: IEEE 1609.2 PQC Amendment (V2X)
description: IEEE vehicular communications security standard update for PQC
notes: IEEE 1609.2 amendment adds ML-KEM and ML-DSA PQC support for vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communications security. Directly affects automotive OEMs and connected vehicle infrastructure.

label: OASIS PKCS#11 Technical Committee
description: OASIS technical committee defining the PKCS#11 HSM programming interface standard
notes: OASIS defines the PKCS#11 standard — the programming interface that every hardware security module implements. PQC mechanisms in v3.2 (ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA) will drive the entire HSM industry PQC adoption.

label: 3GPP Security Working Group
description: 3GPP SA3 — global mobile network security standards working group studying PQC integration
notes: 3GPP SA3 defines global mobile network security standards for 5G and 6G. Actively studying PQC integration into core network protocols. Distinct from ETSI and GSMA. TR 33.841 (PQC Study) approved May 2025.

label: FS-ISAC PQC Migration Guidance
description: Financial Services Information Sharing and Analysis Center PQC migration advisory
notes: FS-ISAC (Sept 2025) warns financial sector has not defined resources for PQC migration. Joint Europol-FS-ISAC report (Jan 2026) provides Quantum Risk Score framework and migration complexity assessment for financial institutions.

label: ASC X9 Financial PKI & PQC Standards
description: Accredited Standards Committee X9 develops cryptographic standards for the financial services industry. X9 Financial PKI (launched June 2025, with DigiCert) provides PQC-enabled certificate infrastructure for banks, payment terminals, and financial enterprises. Key PQC standards: X9.146 (Quantum TLS for financial services), X9 TR-50 (Quantum Techniques in CMS), and the X9 PQC Financial Readiness Needs Assessment (2025).
notes: X9 Financial PKI supports ML-KEM and ML-DSA alongside legacy algorithms for backward compatibility. X9.146 Quantum TLS draft in beta testing (2025). X9 is the primary ANSI-accredited standards body for financial cryptography in the US.

label: PKI Consortium
description: Industry consortium coordinating PQC readiness for PKI operators and certificate authorities
notes: PKI Consortium (pkic.org) organizes annual PQC conferences and publishes PQC migration guidance for certificate authorities. Active since 2013; PQC focus accelerated since 2023. Members include DigiCert, Sectigo, Keyfactor, Entrust. Published hybrid certificate guidance and interoperability testing reports for ML-DSA and composite certificates.

label: Cloud Security Alliance
description: Cloud Security Alliance Quantum-Safe Working Group developing quantum security controls for cloud environments
notes: CSA Quantum-Safe Security Working Group publishes guidance on quantum-safe cloud security controls. CCM v4 includes cryptographic governance controls applicable to PQC migration. CSA also maintains the Cloud Controls Matrix used by cloud providers for compliance attestation. Referenced by CSA quantum-safe guidelines cited in threat profiles CLOUD-001 through CLOUD-004.

label: ITU-T SG17
description: ITU Telecommunication Standardization Sector Study Group 17 on security standards for global telecom PQC transition
notes: ITU-T Study Group 17 (Security) includes Question 14/17 studying PQC for telecommunications networks. Develops X-series security recommendations. Coordinates with 3GPP SA3 and ETSI QSC on telecom PQC standardization. Focus areas include 5G/6G security architecture with PQC integration and quantum-safe key management for network operators.

label: NIST SP 800-208
description: NIST recommendation for stateful hash-based signature schemes (LMS and XMSS)
notes: NIST SP 800-208 specifies the Leighton-Micali Signature (LMS) and eXtended Merkle Signature Scheme (XMSS) for firmware signing and other stateful signature applications. Referenced by CNSA 2.0 as an approved signature algorithm for national security systems. Critical for firmware signing and code signing where stateful signatures are acceptable. Requires careful state management to prevent one-time key reuse.

label: ISO/IEC 19790:2024
description: International standard for security requirements for cryptographic modules — equivalent of FIPS 140-3
notes: ISO/IEC 19790:2024 (3rd edition) defines security requirements for cryptographic modules. International equivalent of NIST FIPS 140-3. Used by JCMVP (Japan), KCMVP (South Korea), and as the basis for EUCC cryptographic module evaluation. PQC algorithm testing requirements are being updated as ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 27 publishes PQC standards. Organizations outside US jurisdiction often require ISO/IEC 19790 rather than FIPS 140-3.

label: ETSI GS QKD Series
description: ETSI Group Specification series for Quantum Key Distribution covering protocols interfaces and network management
notes: ETSI ISG QKD has published 18 specifications covering QKD protocol interfaces (GS QKD 004), key delivery APIs (GS QKD 014), network interoperability (GS QKD 018), and management. QKD complements PQC for defense-in-depth in high-assurance environments. India DST/NQM roadmap recommends hybrid PQC plus QKD. BSI and ANSSI have published joint position papers on QKD vs PQC trade-offs.

label: ANSSI Security Certification
description: French national cybersecurity certification scheme — issued world first PQC product certifications in 2025
notes: ANSSI security certification scheme issued the world first PQC product certifications (Sept-Oct 2025): Thales Smartcard MultiApp 5.2 Premium PQC (EAL5+) and Samsung S3SSE2A. The qualification process will require PQC from 2027 for new product submissions. ANSSI operates under the EUCC scheme and the SOG-IS Mutual Recognition Agreement. Separate from ANSSI as a standardization body (ANSSI-BODY).
library_refs: ANSSI PQC Position Paper;ANSSI PQC Follow-up Paper;ANSSI-PQC-FAQ-2025

label: JCMVP
description: Japanese Cryptographic Module Validation Program — Japan equivalent of NIST CMVP for government procurement
notes: JCMVP (Japanese Cryptographic Module Validation Program) is run by IPA (Information-technology Promotion Agency Japan). Japan equivalent of NIST CMVP/FIPS 140-3 based on ISO/IEC 19790 and JIS X 19790. Mandatory for Japanese government cryptographic module procurement. PQC algorithm integration is expected after CRYPTREC completes its PQC evaluation and publishes updated cipher recommendations.

label: Singapore CSA Quantum-Safe Handbook
description: Singapore Cyber Security Agency national guidelines for quantum-safe transition across all sectors
notes: CSA Singapore published the National Quantum-Safe Handbook providing comprehensive PQC migration guidance for all sectors. Goes beyond MAS financial circular (MAS-CIRCULAR) to cover government, telecom, and technology sectors. Includes algorithm selection guidance aligned with NIST FIPS 203/204/205, migration planning templates, and industry-specific recommendations. Singapore also runs a QKD Sandbox program.

label: Brazil LGPD/ANPD PQC Guidance
description: Brazilian data protection authority ANPD guidance on cryptographic requirements under LGPD with PQC implications
notes: Brazil Lei Geral de Protecao de Dados (LGPD) requires appropriate technical measures including encryption for personal data protection. ANPD (Autoridade Nacional de Protecao de Dados) has published guidance on security measures. While no explicit PQC mandate exists, LGPD long data retention requirements (up to lifetime for health data) create HNDL risk. Brazil is Latin Americas largest economy and a major financial services market. Banco Central do Brasil participates in BIS quantum readiness initiatives.

label: Denmark CFCS PQC Guidance
description: Danish Centre for Cyber Security post-quantum cryptography guidance for government and critical infrastructure
notes: CFCS (Centre for Cybersikkerhed) published PQC migration guidance for Danish government entities and critical infrastructure operators. Denmark is a Five Eyes+ partner and aligns with EU NIS2 and the EU coordinated PQC roadmap (EU-REC-2024-1101) timelines: national roadmaps by Dec 2026, high-risk migration by Dec 2030, full transition by Dec 2035. CFCS is part of the Danish Defence Intelligence Service.

label: NY DFS 23 NYCRR 500
description: New York Department of Financial Services cybersecurity regulation with explicit encryption and key management requirements
notes: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 is one of the most prescriptive US financial cybersecurity regulations. Section 500.15 requires encryption of nonpublic information both in transit and at rest using controls identified by the CISO risk assessment. The Second Amendment (effective November 2025) added enhanced requirements for encryption, key management, and incident response. While no explicit PQC mandate, the encryption requirements create a compliance pathway for PQC adoption as NIST standards mature. Applies to all DFS-regulated entities including banks, insurers, and financial service companies operating in New York.

label: ETSI EN 303 645 (IoT Security)
description: ETSI baseline cybersecurity standard for consumer IoT products with cryptographic requirements affecting long-lived devices
deadline: Ongoing (mandatory under EU RED delegated act from August 2025)
notes: ETSI EN 303 645 establishes baseline cybersecurity provisions for consumer IoT products. Provision 5.1 mandates that all cryptography used must follow best practice and be updateable. The EU Radio Equipment Directive (RED) delegated act makes compliance mandatory for wireless IoT devices sold in the EU from August 2025. PQC relevance: IoT devices with 10-15 year lifespans deployed today will operate well into the CRQC era. The crypto-agility requirement (updatable cryptography) supports future PQC migration. Covers smart home, wearables, connected appliances, and industrial IoT.

label: PQCA
description: Post-Quantum Cryptography Alliance — Linux Foundation project advancing PQC adoption through open-source tooling and community collaboration
notes: Founded 2024 by Linux Foundation with AWS, Cisco, IBM, Google, NVIDIA, SandboxAQ, and others. Houses the OQS project and PQ Code Package. Promotes standardized open-source PQC implementations.

label: PQC Coalition
description: PQC Coalition — MITRE-led cooperative advancing quantum-safe migration across critical infrastructure sectors
notes: Launched by MITRE with members from government agencies and industry. Focuses on practical PQC migration guidance, interoperability testing, and sector-specific transition plans.

label: QED-C
description: Quantum Economic Development Consortium — NIST-established consortium enabling the quantum ecosystem including PQC readiness assessment
notes: Established by NIST in 2018 under the National Quantum Initiative Act. Over 300 members from industry, academia, and government. Develops use cases, standards alignment, and workforce development for quantum technologies including PQC.

label: POPIA
description: South Africa Protection of Personal Information Act — data protection regulation with strong encryption and breach-notification requirements relevant to PQC migration for long-retention data
notes: POPIA Section 19 requires "appropriate, reasonable technical and organisational measures" including encryption for personal information. Long data-retention obligations expose regulated entities to HNDL risk; migration guidance expected from the Information Regulator as NIST PQC suite gains regional traction via SABS.

label: NDPR / NDPA 2023
description: Nigeria Data Protection Regulation + Nigeria Data Protection Act 2023 — jointly enforced by the Nigeria Data Protection Commission (NDPC), with explicit encryption obligations for personal data
notes: NDPA 2023 establishes the NDPC and codifies the prior NDPR. Section 39 mandates appropriate technical measures including encryption. NITDA has signalled alignment with NIST PQC suite for regulated sectors (banking, telecoms). No explicit PQC deadline but HNDL exposure on financial and health data is material.

label: Kenya Data Protection Act 2019
description: Kenya Data Protection Act — enforced by the Office of the Data Protection Commissioner (ODPC); imposes encryption-at-rest and in-transit obligations on data controllers
notes: ODPC Guidance Note on Data Protection by Design (2022) requires state-of-the-art cryptography. The Communications Authority of Kenya is coordinating with ODPC on PQC readiness for the telecoms sector. M-Pesa and other mobile-money rails are high-HNDL-exposure systems.

label: Egypt Personal Data Protection Law
description: Egypt Law No. 151 of 2020 on the Protection of Personal Data — enforced by the Egyptian Data Protection Center (DPC); mandates encryption and security controls for personal data processing
notes: Law 151/2020 came into force in October 2020; executive regulations issued 2024. Article 6 requires controllers to implement encryption proportionate to risk. Egypt Financial Regulatory Authority is tracking NIST PQC adoption; no explicit PQC deadline yet but HNDL risk is material for long-lived government records.

label: African Union Malabo Convention
description: African Union Convention on Cyber Security and Personal Data Protection — pan-continental instrument harmonising cybersecurity and data-protection regimes across AU member states
notes: The Malabo Convention entered into force on 8 June 2023 after Mauritania deposited the 15th instrument of ratification. Article 25 requires member states to adopt appropriate technical measures including encryption. The AU Cybersecurity Expert Group 2025 report highlights quantum-safe transition as a priority for regional harmonisation.
